commit.message:
fix: MSVC compiler compatibility in consteval name-extraction functions

column_field.hpp (tag_to_column_name):
- Add MSVC-specific end delimiters: MSVC __FUNCSIG__ closes the template
  argument with '>' (e.g. tag_to_column_name<struct 'anonymous'::tag>)
  rather than ';'/'_tag]' used in GCC/Clang __PRETTY_FUNCTION__.
  Without this, both end1 and end2 are npos and the static_assert fires.

schema_generator.hpp (extract_type_name):
- Rewrite to use constexpr local variables and ternary expressions in
  place of if/else chains. In a consteval function MSVC instantiates
  per-type, making each find() result a known compile-time constant;
  comparing those against npos in a regular 'if' triggers warning C4127
  ('conditional expression is constant') which /WX promotes to an error.
  Switching to constexpr + ternary eliminates all C4127 warnings.
- Also convert marker_pos/start/end to constexpr so if constexpr can
  guard the npos fallback path cleanly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ol-superset): sqlglot column validation for virtual datasets + SELECT * warnings

Uses sqlglot to parse virtual dataset SQL queries and validate that every
column referenced by a dependent chart actually appears in the query output.
Also warns when virtual datasets use SELECT * or table.* wildcards that
prevent static column analysis.

Changes:

asset_index.py:
- Add sqlglot + sqlglot.expressions imports
- Add VirtualColumnsResult(NamedTuple) with columns: set[str]|None and
  has_wildcard: bool fields
- Add extract_virtual_dataset_columns(sql) — parses outermost SELECT,
  returns exact lowercase column names; returns (None, has_wildcard=True)
  for SELECT */table.* and (None, has_wildcard=False) on parse failure
- DatasetAsset gains virtual_columns: set[str]|None and sql_has_wildcard: bool
- _load_datasets() calls extract_virtual_dataset_columns() for every virtual
  dataset and stores the result on the DatasetAsset

validate.py (section [4/4]):
- 4b: warn when dataset.sql_has_wildcard — surface it as an actionable
  message with the suggestion to replace with an explicit column list
- 4c: for virtual datasets, compare chart column refs against
  virtual_columns (case-insensitive lowercase); skip only when
  virtual_columns is None (wildcard or unparseable)
- 4c: for simple datasets, continue using columns|calculated_columns as before
- Distinguish message wording: 'SQL output' vs 'column list' per type

pyproject.toml:
- Add sqlglot>=20.0 to runtime dependencies

tests/test_asset_index.py (+9 tests, 37 total):
- Updated fixture: virtual dataset uses explicit SELECT columns; added
  separate wildcard dataset (uuid=pqr999, SELECT a.*)
- test_build_asset_index_virtual_dataset: assert virtual_columns parsed
- test_build_asset_index_wildcard_dataset: assert sql_has_wildcard=True
- 8 new extract_virtual_dataset_columns tests covering: simple SELECT,
  aliases, case normalisation, CTE outer SELECT, bare SELECT *, table.*,
  parse error, empty string

Results on real assets: 2 SELECT * warnings for Program_Enrollment_with_user
and marts__mitxonline_course_engagements_daily; section_title genuinely
missing from marts__combined_video_engagements_w_video_counts SQL output
now correctly surfaced.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix CI failures and address code review: EventRow rendering, naming consistency, eager loading (#110)

* Initial plan

* Extract shared components, fix safe navigation bugs, fix AASM state mapping, fix N+1 queries

- Create Shared::StatusBadge — universal status badge for all AASM states
- Create Shared::StatCard — reusable stat/metric card (DRY)
- Create Shared::Web3Address — crypto address formatter with truncation
- Create Shared::DataTable — standardized table wrapper
- Fix 4 safe navigation bugs (&. without method name) in Gateways::Item, Gateways::Index, Gateways::Show, Trees::Show
- Fix Wallets::TransactionRow token_type mapping (mint/burn → carbon_coin/forest_coin)
- Fix Wallets::TransactionRow to use model's explorer_url instead of hardcoded Polygonscan
- Fix missing 'pending' state in BlockchainTransactions::Index and Show status displays
- Fix Clusters::Show alert_severity_class phantom 'high' severity (model only has low/medium/critical)
- Fix Trees::Index to use Tree#charge_percentage model method instead of duplicated calculation
- Fix N+1: Gateways::Item uses latest_gateway_telemetry_log association
- Fix N+1: Clusters::Item uses active_threats? and total_active_trees instead of raw queries
- Fix N+1: Organizations::Show uses total_active_trees counter cache
- Fix N+1: Maintenance::Index uses photos_attachments.size instead of photos.size
- Fix unbounded query: Trees::Show maintenance_history now limited to 20
- Fix unbounded query: Gateways::Show soldier fleet limited to 200
- Fix unbounded query: Clusters::Show gateways limited to 50
- Accept pre-loaded data in Trees::Show, Gateways::Show, Clusters::Show initializers
- Refactor Maintenance::Index to use Shared::Pagination (removed duplicated pagination_bar)
- Refactor Reports::Index and Organizations::Show to use Shared::StatCard

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix AASM state mismatches, add pagination to Wallets::Show, fix N+1 in dashboard, add missing nav items

- Fix Actuators::Show cmd_status_class phantom 'executed' state → use actual AASM states (issued, sent, acknowledged, confirmed, failed)
- Fix Actuators::Card status_led_class phantom 'faulty' state → use actual Actuator AASM state 'maintenance_needed'
- Fix Gateways::Show state_badge_classes missing 'maintenance' and 'faulty' Gateway AASM states
- Fix Contracts::Index status_color — now covers all NaaS contract states (draft, active, fulfilled, breached, cancelled)
- Add pagination to Wallets::Show transaction ledger (was missing despite controller paginating)
- Fix Dashboard N+1: eager load cluster/wallet.tree/user for event feed items
- Preload actuator commands in ActuatorsController index to prevent N+1 in Actuators::Card
- Refactor Reports::CarbonAbsorption and Reports::FinancialSummary to use Shared::StatCard
- Add Maintenance Records link to navigation sidebar (was missing from UI)
- Add System Audits link to navigation sidebar (was missing from Admin section)

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review feedback: fix web3_address truncation guard, battery_color nil fallback, rename variable

- Fix Shared::Web3Address to guard against addresses shorter than truncation length
- Fix Gateways::Show battery_color to default to healthy voltage when no telemetry exists
- Rename ambiguous @maintenance variable to @maintenance_records in TreesController

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract Shared::PhotoCard and MetaRow, use Web3Address/StatCard in more views, fix bugs

- Extract Shared::PhotoCard from Maintenance::PhotoGallery and PhotosPage (removed ~70 lines of duplication)
- Create Shared::MetaRow reusable component for key-value metadata display
- Refactor Contracts::Index to use Shared::StatCard (removed duplicated stat_block method)
- Refactor Settings::Show to use Shared::Web3Address for crypto address display
- Refactor Organizations::Index to use Shared::Web3Address
- Refactor Organizations::Show to use Shared::Web3Address
- Refactor Wallets::Index to use Shared::Web3Address
- Fix Contracts::Show — add missing thead to emission ledger table
- Fix Contracts::Show — add nil guard for tx_hash (previously crashed on nil.first(12))
- Fix Contracts::Show — add empty state for emission history
- Fix Users::Profile — cache @active_identities to avoid 3 separate queries on identities.active
- Fix Maintenance::Show — use @pagy_photos.count instead of @record.photos.size (avoids extra query)

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: fix Web3Address truncation edge case, add DEFAULT_HEALTHY_VOLTAGE_MV constant

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix severity mapping, org-scope firmware stats, extract ActionBadge, use StatCard in dashboard

Critical fixes:
- Fix Alerts::Row severity mapping: 'warning' → 'medium' to match EwsAlert model enum (low/medium/critical)
- Fix Alerts::Index filter badges: non-functional <span> → actual <a> filter links with correct severity names
- Fix Firmwares controller: scope inventory stats to current_user.organization (was leaking cross-org data)
- Fix Users::Profile: cache maintenance_records.count to avoid repeated query

DRY refactoring:
- Refactor Dashboard::Home to use Shared::StatCard (removed duplicated render_stat_card method)
- Refactor Dashboard::EventRow to handle all 3 event types (EwsAlert, BlockchainTransaction, MaintenanceRecord)
- Wire Dashboard::Home to use Dashboard::EventRow (was duplicating event rendering inline)
- Create Shared::ActionBadge — extract audit action badge pattern from AuditLogs::Index and Show
- Replace Contracts::InsuranceStatus with Shared::StatusBadge in insurance_payout_worker (removed duplicated component)

Hygiene:
- Add missing frozen_string_literal to 7 files
- Fix RuboCop empty line offenses

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: simplify Web3Address truncation, fix tx_hash ellipsis, explicit low severity

- Simplify Shared::Web3Address truncation logic (clearer > comparison)
- Fix Wallets::TransactionRow tx_hash ellipsis (only add when hash is actually longer)
- Add explicit 'low' severity case to Clusters::Show alert_severity_class for clarity

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI: remove stale icon/color kwargs from InsurancePayoutWorker EventRow call

The previous session refactored Dashboard::EventRow to derive icon/color
from the event type internally. The InsurancePayoutWorker was still
passing the old :icon and :color keyword arguments, causing ArgumentError
in all 7 insurance payout tests.

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI: use ActionController::Base.helpers for time_ago_in_words in EventRow

When Dashboard::EventRow is rendered via .call from a Sidekiq worker
(InsurancePayoutWorker), there is no view context, so Phlex's helpers
proxy returns nil. Replace with ActionController::Base.helpers which
works regardless of request context.

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: rename latest_telemetry to latest_telemetry_log, fix eager loading

1. Rename Tree#latest_telemetry → Tree#latest_telemetry_log for naming
   consistency with Gateway#latest_gateway_telemetry_log. Updated all
   references: trees/show.rb, oracle_visions_controller.rb, tree_spec.rb.

2. Fix incomplete eager loading in dashboard_controller.rb: change
   .includes(wallet: :tree) to .includes(wallet: { tree: :cluster })
   to match the join path and prevent N+1 when EventRow accesses
   wallet.tree.cluster.

3. Verified @pagy_photos.count in maintenance/show.rb is correct —
   @pagy_photos is a Pagy instance (set by controller), and .count
   returns the cached total without triggering a SQL query.

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve Clang-20 and MSVC compilation errors

column_field.hpp (Clang-20):
- Replace captureless lambda in tag_to_column_name consteval function
  with a ternary expression. Clang-20 correctly rejects references to
  local constexpr variables inside a [] lambda; GCC was permissive.
  Ternary preserves identical min-of-two-finds logic without any capture.

test_dql_extensions.cpp (MSVC):
- Qualify ds_mysql::round and ds_mysql::format with explicit namespace
  in the round/format alias test (line 297). MSVC pulls ::round from
  <cmath> into the global namespace, making unqualified 'round' ambiguous
  when 'using namespace ds_mysql' is also active.

ci.yml (Clang job):
- Use clang-20 instead of clang-18. clang-18 has a known bug where
  __is_base_of returns false for column_field<Tag,T> derived from
  column_field_tag via a base<T> partial specialization; clang-20 is
  correct. Ubuntu noble 24.04 ships clang-20 in its standard repos.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
matched files (#16)

* Match NuRand and NuRandFloat in nurand.c (3/3 functions 100%)

NuRand: Use Schrage's method with explicit division/modulo instead of
single merged expression. Use long k local variable matching DWARF.

NuRandFloat: Use union type-pun (long l / f32 f) instead of integer
cast, matching DWARF's itemp union. Use 1.0f literal and void param.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Match NuVec4Scale and NuVec4MtxTransform in nuvec4.c (3/3 100%)

- NuVec4Scale: Fix parameter semantics (v=dest r3, v0=src r4)
- NuVec4MtxTransform: Use correct types from DWARF (nuvec_s*, numtx_s*),
  add local variables x/y/z/w matching register allocation
- NuVec4MtxInvTransform: Add casts for updated signature

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* matched files

commit.message:
feat: bump default result limits and add truncation signals (#20)

* feat: bump default result limits and add truncation signals

Addresses user feedback that agents hit the 50-match ceiling on search
tools and abandon the approach entirely rather than paginating.

Default bumps:
- helix_search maxMatches: 50 → 100
- azdo_search_log maxMatches: 50 → 100, maxLogsToSearch: 30 → 50
- azdo_builds top: 10 → 20
- helix_find_files maxItems: 30 → 50
- azdo_artifacts top: 50 → 100
- azdo_test_attachments top: 50 → 100

Truncation signals:
- Add Truncated field to LogSearchResult for console log search
- Wire console log truncation through to MCP SearchResult.Truncated
- Add LimitedResults<T> wrapper for azdo_builds, azdo_artifacts,
  azdo_test_results, azdo_test_attachments with truncated flag and
  agent-friendly note when results are capped
- Wire single-log search Truncated to StoppedEarly in cross-step result

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(ai-team): log truncation session and merge decisions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: complete Phase 6 — error handling hardening, loading UX polish, test coverage

Phase 6.1: Backend error handling hardening (Batty)
- Structured JSON error responses across all API routes
- Graceful degradation in orchestrator when individual agents fail
- Timeout handling for Azure OpenAI and Bing Search API calls
- Input validation on itinerary endpoint
- Python logging throughout

Phase 6.2: Loading UX polish (Pris)
- Multi-step progress indicator mirroring backend phases
- CSS-only loading animations
- Skeleton loader for itinerary display
- Polished error states with retry button
- Fade/slide transitions on load
- Accessible (aria-live regions)

Phase 6.3: Error handling test coverage (Zhora)
- API error response tests
- Orchestrator graceful degradation tests
- Agent failure handling tests
- Web search tool error tests

262 backend tests passed, 63 frontend tests passed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Priority 6: remove redundant screenX > 255 check in RenderBGTile() (+6.1%)

Caller already guarantees ppu_cycles_x < 256, so screenX can never
exceed 255. Removing the dead branch allows JIT to better optimize
the inner loop (unrolling/branch elimination).

204.10 -> 216.45 FPS (+6.1%, far exceeded expected <1%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve Clang and MSVC CI build failures

Clang (tests-clang):
- Switch from clang-15 to clang-18 (available in Ubuntu noble's standard
  repos — no extra PPA needed)
- Remove g++-15 install from Clang job: mixing clang-15 with GCC 15's
  stdlib caused std::ranges::empty_view concept resolution failures;
  clang-18 + default GCC 14 stdlib is a well-tested combination

MSVC (tests-msvc) and all compilers:
- Add add_compile_definitions(BOOST_UT_DISABLE_MODULE) in tests/CMakeLists.txt
  Boost.UT v2.1.0 unconditionally emits 'export module boost.ut' when
  __cpp_modules is defined, causing fatal error C3378 under MSVC
  (/std:c++latest). Disabling module mode forces header-only inclusion
  which works with all three compilers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(docs): point installation link to latest release

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: use D3 data joins for all render layers

Replace imperative for-loop DOM creation with D3 .data().join()
enter/update/exit pattern across all four render layers:

- Layer 1 (tree links): data join on LayoutLink[]
- Layer 2 (IC containers + IC nodes): data join on LayoutICContainer[] and LayoutNode[]
- Layer 3 (advisor links + advisor nodes): data join on LayoutLink[] and LayoutNode[]
- Layer 4 (manager nodes): data join on LayoutNode[] with key fn (d => d.id)

Since the full DOM clear (selectAll('*').remove()) runs before each render,
only the enter selection fires today. This establishes the idiomatic D3
pattern required for future incremental/differential rendering where the
clear can be removed and update/exit selections become active.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Introduce SpecCompliance enum for supporting lenient mode (#463)

* Introduce SpecCompliance enum for supporting lenient mode

* fix: allow unknown XML attributes per 3MF spec anyAttribute

The 3MF spec uses xs:anyAttribute on all XML element definitions,
meaning any attribute from any namespace is valid. The previous
validate_attributes() function incorrectly rejected unknown attributes,
causing files from BambuStudio/OrcaSlicer (which add a 'printable'
attribute on <item>) to fail parsing even in lenient mode.

Fixes #462

commit.message:
Priority 9: CPU opcode dispatch Action[256] table (+7.6%)

- Replace switch(opcode) with pre-populated Action[256] opHandlers array
- InitOpHandlers() assigns lambda for each of 256 opcode entries
- Fall-through groups share same Action reference (NOP, HLT, DOP, etc.)
- Result: 189.75 -> 204.10 avg FPS (+7.6%)
- Debug JIT does not fully optimize dense switch to jump table;
  delegate array provides consistent O(1) dispatch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: avoid using HasField on scalar primitives in DataConverter (#985)

* fix: avoid using HasField on scalar primitives in DataConverter

In proto3, standard scalar fields like string_value or int_value do not support `HasField()`. Calling `HasField()` on these fields raises a ValueError. This commit updates `clarifai/runners/utils/data_utils.py` to evaluate the truthiness or explicit default values of these scalar fields instead of using `HasField()`. `HasField()` calls were retained when checking `pb_value` in `Param.from_proto` because `pb_value` is `google.protobuf.Value` and these variants are part of a oneof.

Co-authored-by: patricklundquist <1460278+patricklundquist@users.noreply.github.com>

* Import json module for default value parsing

Added JSON import to handle default values in methods.

* refactor: extract `_has_scalar_set` helper and add `is_old_format` unit tests (#986)

* Initial plan

commit.message:
fix: wire both weight and italic axes for Commit Mono and custom fonts

Commit Mono VF has wght (200–700) and ital (0–1) axes. The single
@font-face declared font-weight:100 900 and font-style:normal only,
leaving the italic axis entirely unused — the browser would synthesise
italic instead of using the real ital axis.

Fix:
- Two @font-face rules for Commit Mono (same file, normal + italic)
  with corrected weight range 200–700 matching the actual fvar table
- applyCustomFont() now also emits two rules for custom fonts so
  variable fonts get the same treatment automatically
- Upload hint updated to recommend variable .woff2

Also reverts the half-built four-slot CSS from the aborted multi-file
upload attempt (font-slot classes) — back to single-file upload.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add penelope.aml — AML Level 3 (BLOOD COMPILE) resonance engine

Complete penelope resonance engine expressed in AML (Arianna Method Language).
Uses BLOOD COMPILE blocks to embed the full penelope.c implementation with
AML ceremony and structure providing the frame.

12 BLOOD blocks: headers, constants, vocab, stopwords, math, model,
forward, dario, tokenizer, training, generation, and BLOOD MAIN.

All C code is functionally identical to penelope.c — same vocabulary,
same model architecture, same algorithms, same output format, same CLI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Record Priority 4 v2 failed attempt (APU int counter, -1.7%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove instruction files that leak into custom agents (#227)

excludeAgent only supports 'code-review' and 'coding-agent' — custom
agent names are silently ignored. This caused project/product/sre
agents to receive coding-agent behavioral instructions (create
branches, write tests, etc.) and break character.

code-review.instructions.md was pure duplication of copilot-instructions.md.
coding-agent.instructions.md content is already in the platform-injected
custom_instruction block.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
